questions.stem_en: A shopkeeper marks the price of an article 25% above the cost price and offers a discount of 12% on the marked price. Find the profit percentage.
questions.stem_hi: एक दुकानदार किसी वस्तु का marked price उसके cost price से 25% अधिक रखता है और marked price पर 12% की discount देता है। profit प्रतिशत क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracts 12 from 25 directly — ignores that markup is on cost but discount is on marked, not on the same base.
options.trap_reason_hi: 12 को 25 से सीधे घटाया — markup cost पर है लेकिन discount marked price पर है, base अलग है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Common error of computing 25-(12% of 25) = 22; close to right approach but wrong base.
options.trap_reason_hi: 25-(12% of 25)=22 के तरीके से निकाला; तरीका सही पर base गलत है।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Picks the discount % itself — confuses discount % with profit %.
options.trap_reason_hi: discount % को ही profit % समझ लिया।

questions.explanation_method_en: Shortcut: Profit% = markup% - discount% - (markup% × discount%)/100 = 25 - 12 - (25 × 12)/100 = 25 - 12 - 3 = 10%. Use this formula directly when both adjustments are small.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Take CP = ₹100. Marked price = 100 × 1.25 = ₹125. Selling price = 125 × (1 - 0.12) = 125 × 0.88 = ₹110. Profit = ₹10 on ₹100 cost = 10%. The two adjustments compound; you cannot simply subtract 12 from 25 because the bases differ — markup is on CP, discount is on MP.
questions.explanation_method_hi: shortcut: profit% = markup% - discount% - (markup% × discount%)/100 = 25 - 12 - (25 × 12)/100 = 25 - 12 - 3 = 10%। दोनों adjustments छोटे हों तो यह formula सीधे लगाएँ।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: मान लीजिए CP = ₹100। Marked price = 100 × 1.25 = ₹125। Selling price = 125 × (1 - 0.12) = 125 × 0.88 = ₹110। Profit = ₹100 पर ₹10 = 10%। दोनों adjustments compound होते हैं — 12 को 25 से सीधे घटाना गलत है क्योंकि base अलग हैं (markup CP पर, discount MP पर)।
